title: Field dental emergency red flags
summary: Focused extreme medical support for field dental emergency red flags in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Dental problems can become face, airway, or bloodstream emergencies. This entry flags what needs urgent help. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Field dental emergency red flags”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Field dental emergency red flags”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Field dental emergency red flags”.
paper and pen. Context: “Field dental emergency red flags”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Field dental emergency red flags»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Field dental emergency red flags»
steps: Escalate for facial swelling, fever, trouble swallowing, trouble breathing, spreading redness, eye swelling, trauma, uncontrolled bleeding, or immune weakness.
Keep mouth clean with safe water when possible.
Do not lance abscesses or pull teeth without qualified care.
Avoid placing aspirin or chemicals on gums.
Record pain start, swelling, fever, trauma, and medicines.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Field dental emergency red flags”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Field dental emergency red flags”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Field dental emergency red flags”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Field dental emergency red flags”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Field dental emergency red flags”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Field dental emergency red flags”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Field dental emergency red flags”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Field dental emergency red flags”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Field dental emergency red flags”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Field dental emergency red flags”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Field dental emergency red flags”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Field dental emergency red flags”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Field dental emergency red flags”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Field dental emergency red flags”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Field dental emergency red flags”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Field dental emergency red flags”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Field dental emergency red flags”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Field dental emergency red flags”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Field dental emergency red flags” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Field dental emergency red flags” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Field dental emergency red flags” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Field dental emergency red flags” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Field dental emergency red flags.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Field dental emergency red flags.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Field dental emergency red flags.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Field dental emergency red flags.

title: Knocked-out tooth transport rule
summary: Focused extreme medical support for knocked-out tooth transport rule in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: A knocked-out permanent tooth may be time-sensitive. The goal is clean handling and urgent dental care, not home dentistry. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
paper and pen. Context: “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Knocked-out tooth transport rule»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Knocked-out tooth transport rule»
steps: Confirm it is not a baby tooth before any reimplantation decision; when unsure, seek dental help.
Handle by the crown, not the root.
Keep it moist using dentist-recommended method or milk/saliva container if professional guidance is unavailable.
Get urgent dental care and record time out.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Knocked-out tooth transport rule”.
